question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getLatestFileDataContents()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a filterQueries object with a whereQuery containing thousands of filter entries to getLatestFileDataContents(), causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to generate a SQL query with thousands of AND conditions that exceeds PostgreSQL's maximum query length, causing a DB error?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/fileDataService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getLatestFileContentsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a whereQuery that causes the final SQL string constructed by getLatestFileContentsQuery() to exceed Node.js's maximum string length, throwing a RangeError that crashes the worker process?
